refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR used the phrase 'shall support achieving,' which can imply a solution approach rather than a pure functional requirement. Replacing 'support achieving' with 'enable achieving' keeps the focus on what the system must do without implying how. The FR is atomic as it addresses a single obligation: achieving the energy efficiency target defined by the customer.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the energy_per_ton metric (energy_used divided by ton_moved) over the agreed evaluation period and verify it meets or is below the customer-defined target E_target.

candidate_dps: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control
Optimize operational parameters to minimize energy use per ton moved
Incorporate adaptive load management to balance energy use and throughput
Design energy recovery and reuse mechanisms within the system
Establish a performance evaluation and adjustment process based on measured energy efficiency
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on monitoring and feedback control, which can be modularized independently from other system functions, supporting decoupled design and clear responsibility boundaries.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and reliable measurement of energy consumption and tonnage moved is feasible
Operational policies and safety plans do not restrict implementation of monitoring and control systems
Energy efficiency targets are realistic and measurable within the evaluation period
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider refining the FR to explicitly include measurement and control aspects to better align with the selected DP and ensure clarity in design focus.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for energy management and monitoring within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time data on energy consumption and system status for monitoring and feedback control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Enables communication with other system modules and external services for control commands and data exchange.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as energy monitoring and feedback logically belong here; minimal diffusion as most related functions are internal.
Moderate containment since energy consumption relates to vehicle operation, but feedback control may require external data, causing diffusion.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not energy monitoring, leading to high diffusion if contained here.
Safety services may use energy data for alerts but do not implement monitoring or control, causing diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP as it specializes in energy management, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion of the real-time energy monitoring and feedback control functionality.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw sensor data may cause some interface complexity.
Integration with Telemetry and Network Link interfaces requires careful design to avoid performance bottlenecks.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision recommended; the current DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module, maintaining clear architectural boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement real-time energy consumption monitoring and feedback control' directly addresses the FR of enabling achievement of the customer-defined energy efficiency target by providing the necessary monitoring and control mechanisms to meet the acceptance criteria of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for raw sensor data may cause some interface complexity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating that the DP is well contained within this IM boundary. External interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) are well defined and support the DP without causing significant diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring the energy_per_ton metric over the evaluation period as specified in the acceptance criteria, leveraging the real-time data provided by the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, as this module specializes in energy management and monitoring, ensuring ownership and responsibility are well defined.

recommended_revisions: No revision recommended; the current DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module, maintaining clear architectural boundaries.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully with clear alignment between the functional requirement, design parameter, and implementation module. The DP directly addresses the FR with appropriate monitoring and control mechanisms, and the IM boundary shows high cohesion and low diffusion, indicating good architectural containment. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and no revisions are recommended. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a stable and well-verified design is prudent before further decomposition.

assumptions: The current DP fully satisfies the FR acceptance criteria for energy efficiency.
The NGHE Solution module is capable of implementing the DP with high cohesion and low diffusion.
Verification methods for energy_per_ton metric are reliable and feasible.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity due to dependency on Vehicle module for raw sensor data.
evidence_needed: Operational data confirming real-time energy consumption monitoring accuracy.
Verification results demonstrating achievement of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target over evaluation period.
